assignments.title: Align both lanes on one unified development-environment protocol
assignments.note: PROVEN 2026-06-30: both lanes aligned on one unified protocol = 4 files (placement=environment-layering.md, exchange=collaboration-protocol.md, + AGENTS.md & CLAUDE.md lane contracts). Claude added CLAUDE.md ## Environment Scale Placement + ## Related Contracts index in the protocol. Codex accepted the framing, added ## Shared Environment Understanding to the protocol, and unified the 'sandbox' wording across all three files. Final root-layer cleanup pass (Claude, user-authorized to touch AGENTS.md): added direct AGENTS.md->protocol reference (closing the last asymmetry), listed layering+protocol+CLAUDE.md in the README framework index, and refreshed stale timestamps (ledger top, protocol Last-updated, CAPABILITY_LAYERS Last-updated all -> 2026-06-30 20:30). Four-way cross-refs now closed both directions. All gates green: check-collaboration + check-secrets + check-lab OK.

assignments.title: Generalize lab to develop any large agent: domain-neutral agent behavior kernel
assignments.note: Promoted a scenario contract's 9 hard-coded guards into a domain-neutral kernel (Layer 7): neutral types + 10 composable policy primitives + ordered DecisionEngine + neutral JSONL eval harness. Neutrality proven by tests/test_kernel_neutrality.py (two unrelated agent chains - infra-ops + research - built inline on the kernel). Kept lean: no resident demo families, no scaffold script; a real agent builds its chain in its own workspaces/ scenario. 29 pure-Python tests; check-secrets + check-lab + check-collaboration OK. Did NOT touch Codex's git-excluded support scenario contract - handoff asks Codex to optionally adopt the kernel there. On branch claude/neutral-agent-kernel, not merged. Awaiting Codex review.

assignments.title: Fix fail-open security gates when ripgrep is missing or errors
assignments.note: Claude found that check-secrets and check-sandbox failed open when rg was absent from PATH. Codex review found the same fail-open family still applied when rg existed but exited with a scanner error. This branch now probes for rg and treats rg status 0 as finding detected, status 1 as no match, and any other status as a hard gate failure. Support workspace fixes are intentionally out of scope for this tracked PR and remain local-only until promoted separately.

assignments.title: Support-scenario eval: JSONL scenario fixtures + batch eval runner
assignments.note: Done: 11 JSONL scenarios cover every contract branch; batch runner emits pass/fail + tool-vs-escalation confusion matrix; 4 new tests. Full suite 20/20 pass (16 Codex + 4 mine). New files only, did NOT touch Codex's contract harness. Eval outputs (.gitignore'd) do not pollute git. Awaiting Codex review pass.
